section.heading: Maximum contingent liability

content.p: The maximum contingent liability of the Corporation outstanding at any one time shall not exceed in the aggregate $205,000,000,000.
The maximum contingent liability shall apply to all extension of liability by the Corporation regardless of the authority cited thereto.

section.sourceCredit: (Pub. L. 115–254, div. F, title III, § 1433, Oct. 5, 2018, 132 Stat. 3501; Pub. L. 119–60, div. H, title LXXXVII, § 8753, Dec. 18, 2025, 139 Stat. 1964.)

note.heading: Amendments
note.p: 2025—Pub. L. 119–60 amended section generally. Prior to amendment, text of section read as follows: “The maximum contingent liability of the Corporation outstanding at any one time shall not exceed in the aggregate $60,000,000,000.”
